title: Modport item not found with -fno-inline in --hierarchical child compilations
body:
## Bug

With `-fno-inline`, `--hierarchical` child compilations fail with "Modport item not found" errors. Either flag alone works fine — only the combination breaks.

`stripOptionsForChildRun()` does not strip inline optimization flags, so `-fno-inline` leaks into the generated `*__hierMkArgs.f` child args files. When a hier_block's internal non-hier sub-module uses modport ports, the child compilation runs without inlining and modport resolution fails.

### Reproducer

```systemverilog
interface simple_if (input clk);
  logic [7:0] data;
  modport sender  (input clk, output data);
  modport receiver(input clk, input  data);
endinterface

module inner(
  simple_if.receiver in,
  simple_if.sender   out
);
  assign out.data = in.data + 8'd1;
endmodule

module sub(
  input wire clk,
  input wire [7:0] in,
  output wire [7:0] out
); /*verilator hier_block*/

  simple_if in_if (.clk(clk));
  simple_if out_if(.clk(clk));
  assign in_if.data = in;
  assign out = out_if.data;
  inner i_inner(.in(in_if), .out(out_if));
endmodule

module t(input wire clk);
  wire [7:0] out;
  sub i_sub(.clk(clk), .in(8'd42), .out(out));
endmodule
```

```bash
# FAIL: -fno-inline leaks into Vsub__hierMkArgs.f child compilation
verilator --cc --hierarchical -fno-inline repro.v

# PASS: --hierarchical alone
verilator --cc --hierarchical repro.v

# PASS: -fno-inline alone
verilator --cc -fno-inline repro.v
```

### Error output

```
%Error: repro.v:8:26: Modport item not found: 'clk'
                     : ... note: In instance 'sub.i_inner'
    8 |   modport sender  (input clk, output data);
      |                          ^~~
%Error: repro.v:8:38: Modport item not found: 'data'
%Error: repro.v:9:26: Modport item not found: 'clk'
%Error: repro.v:9:38: Modport item not found: 'data'
```

Inspecting `Vsub__hierMkArgs.f` confirms `-fno-inline` is present in the child args.

### Failing tests (13)

All hier_block tests that use `t_hier_block.v` (which has interface modports):

`t_hier_block`, `t_hier_block_binary`, `t_hier_block_cmake`, `t_hier_block_cmake_protect`, `t_hier_block_prot_lib`, `t_hier_block_sc`, `t_hier_block_trace`, `t_hier_block_trace_fst`, `t_hier_block_trace_fst_sc`, `t_hier_block_trace_sc`, `t_hier_block_treefork`, `t_hier_block_vlt`, plus multithread variants.

Hier_block tests with their own `.v` files without modports (18 tests) pass fine.

### Root cause

`V3Options::stripOptionsForChildRun()` strips flags like `--hierarchical`, `--build`, `-G`, `-pvalue`, etc. from child compilation args, but does not strip inline optimization flags. All 6 variants (`-finline`, `-fno-inline`, `-finline-funcs`, `-fno-inline-funcs`, `-finline-funcs-eager`, `-fno-inline-funcs-eager`) should be stripped since child compilations need default inlining for correct modport/interface resolution.

### Proposed fix

Add prefix matches in the `!forTop` block of `stripOptionsForChildRun()`:

```cpp
if (VString::startsWith(opt, "finline")) return 1;
if (VString::startsWith(opt, "fno-inline")) return 1;
```

This is similar to previous fixes in the same function (#5741, #6572).

### Related

- #5741 / #6572 — previous bugs in `stripOptionsForChildRun()` not filtering child args correctly.
- Found during the `-fno-inline` audit for #1539 (post-scope inliner).

### Version

Tested on master at `bb0e1c8c6`.
